Petition (1) To Invalidate First Trust Amendment To 2008 Restatement And Amendment To Williams Family Trust; (2) To Invalidate Improper Transfers To Co-Trustee Due To Undue Influence And Elder Abuse; (3) Invalidate Transfers To And Actions Of Respondent In Capacity As Attorney In Fact; (4) To Invalidate Transfers To Respondent Co Trustee As Self Dealing And Elder Abuse; (5) For Constructive Trust; (6) For Recovery Of Trust Property And Accounting; (7) For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty; (8) For Removal Of Respondent
Off Calendar Jan-09-2023 Continued To Feb-06-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom: Patrick Miller, Esq. for Brian Williams (Petitioner/Objector). Ciaran O'Sullivan, Esq. for Vivian Williams (Petitioner).

Mr. O'Sullivan stated that the parties were remiss in notifying the court examiner regarding the settlement efforts, that they are very close to settlement, and that they jointly are requesting a continuance. Mr. Miller stated that the parties are close to settlement with the assistance of Betty Epstein, that they have crafted an agreement, and that they need answers from one deposit/institution.

At the conclusion of comments, the petition to invalidate first trust amendment to 2008 restatement, etc., the petition for return of trust property, and the status of mediation are continued to February 6, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. Counsel to notify the probate examiner as soon as possible if the matters settle.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Choi. Matter not reported.

Petition (1) To Invalidate First Trust Amendment To 2008 Restatement And Amendment To Williams Family Trust; (2) To Invalidate Improper Transfers To Co-Trustee Due To Undue Influence And Elder Abuse; (3) Invalidate Transfers To And Actions Of Respondent In Capacity As Attorney In Fact; (4) To Invalidate Transfers To Respondent Co Trustee As Self Dealing And Elder Abuse; (5) For Constructive Trust; (6) For Recovery Of Trust Property And Accounting; (7) For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty; (8) For Removal Of Respondent
Off Calendar Dec-05-2022 Continued To Jan-09-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sean P. Miller, via Zoom, for Brian Williams, Petitioner/Objector; Ciaran O'Sullivan, in court, for Vivian Williams, Petitioner.

Mr. O'Sullivan reports that he believes that the parties have reached a settlement agreement and asks that the court continue this matter for thirty (30) days to allow the parties to finalize the agreement. Mr. Williams concurs with Mr. O'Sullivan's report and asks that the court set this matter for trial and offers advocacy for his request. The court advises that it is not ready to set this matter for trial at this time but will be continuing the subject matters for the time requested and intends to set the matter for trial if the settlement agreement has not been confirmed at that time.

The subject matters are continued to Jan-09-2023 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. Counsel is instructed to notify the court by Jan-03-2023, if the settlement is finalized before then.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

Off Calendar Feb-16-2022 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Limiting Discovery Of Personal Bank Accounts Held At Citibank, N.A., And Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-16-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ciaran O'Sullivan, in person, for Vivian Williams, Petitioner; Patrick Miller, via ZOOM, for Brian Williams, Objector.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions regarding the court's tentative ruling. The court adopts its tentative ruling however, it is striking the ruling regarding sanctions. See order for details.

The court and the Parties discuss mediation and the Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than May-16-2022. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by May-31-2022. The Parties are to return on Jun-13-2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. The parties are to monitor the court's register of actions to obtain a copy of the minute order referring case to pro bono mediation.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: J. Jeung. Court Reporter: Gina Minnis, CSR#: 11996; E: gina.csr@gmail.com; T: 831.649.3110.
